message:
test(fuzz): add PcsGroupState epoch-ratchet state-machine fuzz target (resolves m05g5)

PcsGroupState (pcs.rs:138-410) is the TreeKEM-inspired group key
agreement for sensitive zones (z:owner, z:private). Post-compromise
security requires that advance_epoch produces a NEW zone_key and
remove_member ratchets so the removed peer cannot derive future keys.
A regression in the ratchet would let a compromised peer continue to
read post-rekey traffic.

NOT covered by existing fuzz.

Seven properties driven over a constructed PcsGroupState:
- constructor validation: empty/oversized/duplicate rejected
- member_count == members().len()
- is_member completeness over constructed members + non-member returns
  false
- derive_zone_key determinism on the same state
- advance_epoch ratchet: epoch+1 AND zone_key changes (PCS invariant)
- remove_member: epoch advances, count decreases, target no longer
  member
- add_member: epoch advances, count increases, new node is member

Once-gated regression anchors:
- (a) empty group → EmptyGroup
- (b) PCS_MAX_GROUP_SIZE+1 → GroupTooLarge with correct (size, max)
- (c) duplicate node_id → DuplicateMember(node_id)
- (d) advance_epoch zone_key changes — the load-bearing PCS invariant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
